notes: Initial hand-curated catalog for hsapi Phase 2.
Prefer 2026-03 date-versioned endpoints where HubSpot publishes them.
Keep legacy v3/v4 endpoints where current HubSpot docs still expose those paths or where date-versioned docs are incomplete.
Mutating typed commands should require --yes; destructive schema operations require an extra danger flag.

surfaces.name: marketing.ctas.javascript_sdk
surfaces.scopeNotes: HubSpot documents CTAs as a catalog-only browser JavaScript SDK surface under window.HubSpotCallsToActions, not as a bearer-token REST endpoint. Do not model this as an hsapi command or HubSpot API request.

surfaces.name: automation.workflows.docs
surfaces.scopeNotes: Workflows are documented here because HubSpot's workflow and custom-action model spans several older and newer guides.

surfaces.name: automation.sequences.docs
surfaces.scopeNotes: Sequences keep this guide as a docs-only surface; the REST list/get/enroll/status paths are tracked as endpoint rows.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.token

args.name: client-id-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client ID.

args.name: client-secret-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client secret.

args.name: code-env
args.description: Env var name holding the authorization code.

args.name: yes
args.description: Confirm and execute the mutation. Omit to get a blocked preview.

endpoints.scopeNotes: OAuth authorization-code exchange. Sends client_id, client_secret, code, grant_type, and redirect_uri as form URL-encoded body fields. Response token values are redacted unless --show-secrets is used.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.refresh

args.name: client-id-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client ID.

args.name: client-secret-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client secret.

args.name: refresh-token-env
args.description: Env var name holding the refresh token.

args.name: yes
args.description: Confirm and execute the mutation. Omit to get a blocked preview.

endpoints.scopeNotes: OAuth refresh-token exchange. Response token values are redacted unless --show-secrets is used.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.introspect

args.name: client-id-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client ID.

args.name: client-secret-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client secret.

args.name: token
args.description: Token value (prefer --token-env to keep secrets out of argv).

args.name: token-env
args.description: Env var name holding the token.

endpoints.scopeNotes: Returns token metadata, user, hub, scopes, and app identifiers. Token-like values are redacted unless --show-secrets is used.

endpoints.name: auth.oauth.revoke

args.name: client-id-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client ID.

args.name: client-secret-env
args.description: Env var name holding the client secret.

args.name: token
args.description: Token value (prefer --token-env to keep secrets out of argv).

args.name: token-env
args.description: Env var name holding the token.

args.name: danger-revoke-token
args.description: Required acknowledgment: revocation kills the token.

args.name: yes
args.description: Confirm and execute the mutation. Omit to get a blocked preview.

endpoints.scopeNotes: Revokes an OAuth token. Requires --danger-revoke-token and --yes.

endpoints.name: forms.secure_submit

args.name: portalId

args.name: yes
args.description: Confirm and execute the mutation. Omit to get a blocked preview.

endpoints.scopeNotes: Authenticated api.hsforms.com endpoint. HubSpot docs say authentication is required but omit the auth header in the cURL example; the CLI only sends the selected portal bearer token to the fixed secure form submission host/path.

args.name: yes

endpoints.name: quotes.status
endpoints.scopeNotes: Convenience read preset: returns hs_status, hs_title, hs_expiration_date, hs_quote_amount, hs_quote_link (+ any --properties extras).

args.name: properties
args.description: Extra properties to include beyond the status preset.

endpoints.name: quotes.publish
endpoints.scopeNotes: There is no dedicated publish endpoint: publishing is an hs_status transition on the quote object. Sets APPROVAL_NOT_NEEDED (publishes immediately on portals without quote approvals) or PENDING_APPROVAL with --request-approval. HubSpot rejects the transition if the quote is incomplete or expired.

args.name: request-approval
args.description: Set hs_status PENDING_APPROVAL (portals with quote approvals) instead of APPROVAL_NOT_NEEDED.

args.name: yes
args.description: Confirm and execute the mutation. Omit to get a blocked preview.

endpoints.name: quotes.recall
endpoints.scopeNotes: Returns the quote to DRAFT (takes the published web quote offline).

args.name: yes
args.description: Confirm and execute the mutation. Omit to get a blocked preview.

endpoints.name: owners.list
endpoints.scopeNotes: Owners list supports email, after, limit, and archived query parameters. Owner records map HubSpot users to CRM ownership properties.

endpoints.name: owners.get
endpoints.scopeNotes: Owner get accepts idProperty=id (default) or userId. Archived owners need archived=true.

endpoints.name: analytics.reports
endpoints.scopeNotes: Legacy analytics reads (breakdownBy: totals, sessions, sources, geolocation, pages, ...; timePeriod: total, daily, weekly, monthly, summarize/daily...). Requires start/end query params for ranged reads.

endpoints.name: engagements.v1.paged
endpoints.scopeNotes: Legacy engagement reads; path verified live 2026-06-11. Some note/task fields (source, queue membership) only exist on v1.

endpoints.name: engagements.v1.create
endpoints.scopeNotes: Prefer the modern CRM engagement objects (notes/tasks/calls/emails/meetings) for new writes.
